functions.name: xpart.matched_gaussian.generate_matched_gaussian_bunch
documentation:
Generate a matched Gaussian bunch.

Parameters
----------
line : xpart.Line
    Line for which the bunch is generated.
num_particles : int
    Number of particles to be generated.
nemitt_x : float
    Normalized emittance in the horizontal plane (in m rad).
nemitt_y : float
    Normalized emittance in the vertical plane (in m rad).
sigma_z : float
    RMS bunch length in meters.
total_intensity_particles : float
    Total intensity of the bunch in particles.

Returns
-------
part : xpart.Particles
    Particles object containing the generated particles.

functions.name: xpart.matched_gaussian.split_scheme
documentation:
Distribute the filling scheme between the processes, i.e. assign to each
processor its bunches

modules.name: xpart.longitudinal.pdf_integrators_2d
documentation: This file is part of the Xpart Package.   #

functions.name: xpart.longitudinal.pdf_integrators_2d.quad2d
documentation:
Integrate 2D function f=f(x,y) over interval [xmin,xmax] and
between contours [-ylimits(x), ylimits(x)] using the numerical
scipy.integrate.dblquad integration method.

functions.name: xpart.longitudinal.pdf_integrators_2d.compute_zero_quad
documentation:
Compute the zeroth moment of the distribution function psi from
xmin to xmax between the contours ylimit_min and ylimit_max
(e.g. an RFBucket separatrix) using the numerical
scipy.integrate.dblquad integration method.

Arguments:
    - psi: 2D distribution function with two arguments x and y
    - ylimit_min, ylimit_max: contour functions yielding the lower
      and the upper y(x) limit for given x
    - xmin, xmax: lower and upper limit in the first argument of psi

functions.name: xpart.longitudinal.pdf_integrators_2d.compute_mean_quad
documentation:
Compute the first moment of the distribution function psi from
xmin to xmax between the contours ylimit_min and ylimit_max
(e.g. an RFBucket separatrix) using the numerical
scipy.integrate.dblquad integration method.

Arguments:
    - psi: 2D distribution function with two arguments x and y
    - ylimit_min, ylimit_max: contour functions yielding the lower
      and the upper y(x) limit for given x
    - xmin, xmax: lower and upper limit in the first argument of psi
    - direction: 'x' or 'y' for calculating the mean in the first
      or second argument of psi, respectively (default: 'x')

functions.name: xpart.longitudinal.pdf_integrators_2d.compute_var_quad
documentation:
Compute the second moment (variance) with respect to the x or
y direction of the distribution function psi from xmin to xmax
between the contours ylimit_min and ylimit_max (e.g. an RFBucket
separatrix) using the numerical scipy.integrate.dblquad integration
method.

Arguments:
    - psi: 2D distribution function with two arguments x and y
    - ylimit_min, ylimit_max: contour functions yielding the lower
      and the upper y(x) limit for given x
    - xmin, xmax: lower and upper limit in the first argument of psi
    - direction: 'x' or 'y' for calculating the standard deviation
      in the first or second argument of psi, respectively
      (default: 'x')

functions.name: xpart.longitudinal.pdf_integrators_2d.compute_cov_quad
documentation:
Compute the second moments (covariance matrix entries) of the
distribution function psi from xmin to xmax between the contours
ylimit_min and ylimit_max (e.g. an RFBucket separatrix) using the
numerical scipy.integrate.dblquad integration method.
For x the first and y the second argument of psi, return the tuple
(variance(x), covariance(x,y), variance(y)).

Arguments:
    - psi: 2D distribution function with two arguments x and y
    - ylimits: contour function yielding the y(x) limit for given x
    - xmin, xmax: lower and upper limit in the first argument of psi

functions.name: xpart.longitudinal.pdf_integrators_2d.compute_zero_cumtrapz
documentation:
Compute the zeroth moment of the distribution function psi from
xmin to xmax between the contours ylimit_min and ylimit_max
(e.g. an RFBucket separatrix) using the numerical
scipy.integrate.cumtrapz integration method.

Arguments:
    - psi: 2D distribution function with two arguments x and y
    - ylimit_min, ylimit_max: contour functions yielding the lower
      and the upper y(x) limit for given x
    - xmin, xmax: lower and upper limit in the first argument of psi
    - n_samples: integer number of sampling points for integration

functions.name: xpart.longitudinal.pdf_integrators_2d.compute_mean_cumtrapz
documentation:
Compute the first moment of the distribution function psi from
xmin to xmax between the contours ylimit_min and ylimit_max
(e.g. an RFBucket separatrix) using the numerical
scipy.integrate.cumtrapz integration method.

Arguments:
    - psi: 2D distribution function with two arguments x and y
    - ylimit_min, ylimit_max: contour functions yielding the lower
      and the upper y(x) limit for given x
    - xmin, xmax: lower and upper limit in the first argument of psi
    - direction: 'x' or 'y' for calculating the mean in the first
      or second argument of psi, respectively (default: 'x')
    - n_samples: integer number of sampling points for integration

functions.name: xpart.longitudinal.pdf_integrators_2d.compute_var_cumtrapz
documentation:
Compute the second moment (variance) with respect to the x or
y direction of the distribution function psi from xmin to xmax
between the contours ylimit_min and ylimit_max
(e.g. an RFBucket separatrix) using the numerical
scipy.integrate.cumtrapz integration method.

Arguments:
    - psi: 2D distribution function with two arguments x and y
    - ylimit_min, ylimit_max: contour functions yielding the lower
      and the upper y(x) limit for given x
    - xmin, xmax: lower and upper limit in the first argument of psi
    - direction: 'x' or 'y' for calculating the mean in the first
      or second argument of psi, respectively (default: 'x')
    - n_samples: integer number of sampling points for integration

functions.name: xpart.longitudinal.pdf_integrators_2d.compute_cov_cumtrapz
documentation:
Compute the second moments (covariance matrix entries) of the
distribution function psi from xmin to xmax between the contours
ylimit_min and ylimit_max (e.g. an RFBucket separatrix) using the
numerical scipy.integrate.cumtrapz integration method.
For x the first and y the second argument of psi, return the tuple
(variance(x), covariance(x,y), variance(y)).

Arguments:
    - psi: 2D distribution function with two arguments x and y
    - ylimit_min, ylimit_max: contour functions yielding the lower
      and the upper y(x) limit for given x
    - xmin, xmax: lower and upper limit in the first argument of psi
    - n_samples: integer number of sampling points for integration

functions.name: xpart.longitudinal.pdf_integrators_2d.compute_std_romberg
documentation:
Compute the standard deviation of the distribution function psi
from xmin to xmax along the contours ylimits using numerical
integration methods.
## ==> not yet adapted to above quad and cumtrapz approaches,
##     works as Kevin had previously defined it in the RFBucketMatcher
##     (slightly corrected to make this function work here)

modules.name: xpart.longitudinal.rfbucket_matching
documentation: This file is part of the Xpart Package.   #

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rfbucket_matching.RFBucketMatcher.get_moment_integrators
documentation:
Return moment integrators from
cobra_functions.pdf_integrators_2d according to the chosen
self.integrationmethod. Allows to change integration method
for RFBucket matching.

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rfbucket_matching.RFBucketMatcher.generate
documentation:
Generate a 2d phase space of n_particles particles randomly distributed
according to the particle distribution function psi within the region
[xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax].

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rfbucket_matching.StationaryDistribution._psi
documentation:
Define the distribution value for the given H, the output
lies in the interval [0,1]. This is the central function to
be implemented by stationary distributions.

classes.name: xpart.longitudinal.rfbucket_matching.ThermalDistribution
documentation:
Thermal Boltzmann distribution psi ~ exp(-H/H0).
For a quadratic harmonic oscillator Hamiltonian this gives the
bi-Gaussian phase space distribution.

classes.name: xpart.longitudinal.rfbucket_matching.QGaussianDistribution
documentation:
Specific Tsallis q-Gaussian distribution for q=3/5 for now,
leading to psi ~ (1 - H/H0)^2, this may be generalised.

classes.name: xpart.longitudinal.rfbucket_matching.ParabolicDistribution
documentation:
The parabolic profile distribution is a specific case of the
present implementation of the q-Gaussian distribution for n = 1/2,
psi ~ sqrt(1 - H/H0).
For a quadratic harmonic oscillator Hamiltonian this distribution
provides a parabolic line density.

classes.name: xpart.longitudinal.rfbucket_matching.WaterbagDistribution
documentation:
The waterbag distribution has a constant Hamiltonian distribution
until a cutoff, psi ~ Theta(H - H0) with Theta the Heaviside
step function.

functions.name: xpart.test_helpers.retry
documentation:
A decorator to be used on a flaky test. The test will be rerun until no
exception defined in `on` is thrown, up to `n_times`. If `on` is
unspecified, then the functionality of `flaky_assertions` is expected to be
applied: the test will only be retried on AssertionErrors occurring within
a `with flaky_assertions(): ...` statement.

modules.name: xpart.longitudinal.generate_longitudinal
documentation: This file is part of the Xpart Package.   #

functions.name: xpart.longitudinal.generate_longitudinal.generate_longitudinal_coordinates
documentation:
Generate longitudinal coordinates matched to given RF parameters (non-linar
bucket).

Parameters
----------
line: xline.Line
    Line for which the longitudinal coordinates are generated.
num_particles: int
    Number of particles to be generated.
distribution: str
    Distribution of the particles. Possible values are `gaussian` and
    `parabolic`.
sigma_z: float
    RMS bunch length in meters.
engine: str
    Engine to be used for the generation. Possible values are `pyheadtail`
    and `single-rf-harmonic`.
return_matcher: bool
    If True, the matcher object is returned.

Returns
-------
zeta: np.ndarray
    Longitudinal position of the generated particles.
delta: np.ndarray
    Longitudinal momentum deviation of the generated particles.
matcher: object
    Matcher object used for the generation. Returned only if
    `return_matcher` is True.

functions.name: xpart.transverse_generators.polar.generate_2D_polar_grid
documentation:
Generate a 2D polar grid.

Parameters
----------
r_range : tuple
    Range of the radial coordinate.
r_grid : np.ndarray
    Grid of the radial coordinate.
dr : float
    Step of the radial coordinate.
nr : int
    Number of points of the radial coordinate.
theta_range : tuple
    Range of the angular coordinate.
theta_grid : np.ndarray
    Grid of the angular coordinate.
dtheta : float
    Step of the angular coordinate.
ntheta : int
    Number of points of the angular coordinate.

Returns
-------
a1 : np.ndarray
    First normalized coordinate.
a2 : np.ndarray
    Second normalized coordinate.
r_all : np.ndarray
    Radial coordinate.
theta_all : np.ndarray
    Angular coordinate.

functions.name: xpart.transverse_generators.polar.generate_2D_uniform_circular_sector
documentation:
Generate a 2D uniform circular sector.

Parameters
----------

    Number of particles to be generated.
r_range : tuple
    Range of the radial coordinate.
theta_range : tuple
    Range of the angular coordinate.

Returns
-------
a1 : np.ndarray
    First normalized coordinate.
a2 : np.ndarray
    Second normalized coordinate.
r_all : np.ndarray
    Radial coordinate.

    Angular coordinate.

functions.name: xpart.transverse_generators.pencil.generate_2D_pencil
documentation:
Generate a 2D pencil beam distribution.

Parameters
----------
num_particles : int
    Number of particles to be generated.
pos_cut_sigmas : float
    Position cut in sigmas.
dr_sigmas : float
    Radius of the pencil beam in sigmas.
side : str
    Side of the pencil beam. Can be '+', '-' or '+-'.

Returns
-------
x1 : np.ndarray
    First normalized coordinate.
x2 : np.ndarray
    Second normalized coordinate.

functions.name: xpart.transverse_generators.pencil.generate_2D_pencil_with_absolute_cut
documentation:
Generate a 2D pencil beam distribution with an absolute cut.

Parameters
----------
line: xtrack.Line
    Line for which the coordinates are generated.
num_particles : int
    Number of particles to be generated.
plane : str
    Plane of the pencil beam. Can be 'x' or 'y'.
absolute_cut : float
    Absolute cut in meters.
dr_sigmas : float
    Radius of the pencil beam in sigmas.
side : str
    Side of the pencil beam. Can be '+' or '-'.

Returns
-------
x1 : np.ndarray
    First normalized coordinate.
x2 : np.ndarray
    Second normalized coordinate.

modules.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket
documentation: This file is part of the Xpart Package.   #

functions.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.attach_clean_buckets
documentation:
Wrap an rf_parameter_changing_method (that changes relevant RF
parameters, i.e. Kick attributes). Needs to be an instance method,
presumably an RFSystems instance (hence the self argument in
cleaned_rf_parameter_changing_method).
In detail, attaches a call to the rfsystems_instance.clean_buckets
method after calling the wrapped function.

classes.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.__init__
documentation:
Implements only the leading order momentum compaction factor.

Arguments:
- mass_kg is the mass of the particle type in the beam
- charge_coulomb is the charge of the particle type in the beam
- z_offset determines the centre for the bucket interval
over which the root finding (of the electric force field to
calibrate the separatrix Hamiltonian value to zero) is done.
z_offset is per default determined by the zero crossing located
closest to z == 0.

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.z_ufp
documentation:
Return the (left-most) unstable fix point on the z axis
within self.interval .

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.z_sfp
documentation:
Return the (left-most) stable fix point on the z axis.
within self.interval .

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.z_ufp_separatrix
documentation:
Return the (left-most) unstable fix point at the outermost
separatrix of the bucket.
(i.e. a bucket boundary defining unstable fix point)

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.z_sfp_extr
documentation:
Return the (left-most) absolute extremal stable fix point
within the bucket.

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.z_left
documentation: Return the left bucket boundary within self.interval .

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.z_right
documentation: Return the right bucket boundary within self.interval .

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.R
documentation: '''Return the left bucket boundary within self.interval .'''
'''Return the right bucket boundary within self.interval .'''

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.eta0
documentation: should make use of eta functionality of LongitudinalMap at some point

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.Q_s
documentation:
Linear synchrotron tune for small amplitudes i.e., in the
center of the bucket. Analytical formula neglects any
added forces / potentials via add_fields.

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.add_fields
documentation:
Include additional (e.g. non-RF) effects to this RFBucket.
Use this interface for adding space charge influence etc.
to the bucket parameters and shape.

Arguments:
- add_forces are additional electric force fields to be added
on top of the RF electric force field.
add_forces is expected to be an iterable of functions of z,
in units of Coul*Volt/metre.
- add_potentials are additional electric potential energies
to be added on top of the RF electric potential energy.
add_potentials is expected to be an iterable of functions of z,
in units of Coul*Volt.

Bucket shape parameters z_ufp, z_sfp, z_left and z_right are
recalculated.

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.rf_force
documentation: FORCE FIELDS AND POTENTIALS OF MULTI-HARMONIC ACCELERATING BUCKET

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.total_force
documentation:
Return the total electric force field including
- the acceleration offset and
- the additional electric force fields (provided via
self.add_nonRF_influences),
evaluated at position z in units of Coul*Volt/metre.

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.rf_potential
documentation:
Return the RF electric potential energy including the linear
acceleration slope (if acceleration == True).

Arguments:
    - V: list of voltages for each harmonic
    - h: list of harmonics
    - dphi: list of phase offsets for each harmonic
    - p_increment: momentum increase per turn
    - acceleration: whether to superimpose the linear
      acceleration slope (induced by p_increment, default=True)
    - offset: boolean whether the potential energy should be
      shifted to zero at the unstable fix point enclosing
      the separatrix of the RF bucket (default=True).
'''Return the electric force field of a single harmonic
RF element as a function of z in units of Coul*Volt/metre.
'''Return the stationary total electric force field of
superimposed RF elements (multi-harmonics) as a function of z.
Parameters are taken from RF parameters of this
Adds the additional electric force fields (provided via
'''Return stationary total electric force field of
force fields as a function of z in units of Coul*Volt/metre.
for V, h, dphi in zip(self.V, self.h, self.dphi))
return (sum(harmonics) + sum(f(z) for f in self._add_forces
@deprecated('--> Replace with "total_force" as soon as possible.\n')
'''Return the total electric force field including
- the additional electric force fields (provided via
evaluated at position z in units of Coul*Volt/metre.

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.total_potential
documentation:
Return the total electric potential energy including
- the linear acceleration slope and
- the additional electric potential energies (provided via
self.add_nonRF_influences),
evaluated at position z in units of Coul*Volt.

Note:
Adds a potential energy offset: this relocates the extremum
(defining the unstable fix point UFP of the bucket)
to obtain zero potential energy at the UFP.
Thus the Hamiltonian value of the separatrix is calibrated
to zero.

Arguments:
    - make_convex: multiplies by sign(eta) for plotting etc.
      To see a literal 'bucket structure' in the sense of
      always having a local maximum in the Hamiltonian topology
      where the stable fix points are located, set
      make_convex=True in order to return
      sign(eta)*hamiltonian(z, dp).
    - offset: boolean whether the potential energy should be
      shifted to zero at the unstable fix point enclosing
      the separatrix of the RF bucket (default=True).

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.zero_crossings
documentation:
Determine roots of f along x.
If x is not explicitely given, take stationary bucket interval.
'''Return the electric potential energy of a single harmonic
RF element as a function of z in units of Coul*Volt.
'''Return the stationary total electric potential energy of
superimposed RF elements (multi-harmonics) as a function of z.
Parameters are taken from RF parameters of this
Adds the additional electric potential energies
'''Return stationary total electric potential energy of
electric potentials as a function of z
for V, h, dphi in zip(self.V, self.h, self.dphi))
return (sum(harmonics) + sum(pot(z) for pot in self._add_potentials

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket._get_bucket_boundaries
documentation:
Return the bucket boundaries as well as the whole list
of acceleration voltage roots, (z_left, z_right, z_roots).

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket._get_zsfp_and_zufp
documentation:
Return (z_sfp, z_ufp),
where z_sfp is the z location of the stable fix points,
and z_ufp is the z location of the unstable fix points
belonging to this RF bucket.

A stationary RF bucket has the right-most UFP overlapping
with the adjacent RF bucket's separatrix, while for an
ac-/decelerating RF bucket one of the two out-most UFP always
belongs to the separatrix of the adjacent RF bucket. This UFP
will be discarded (although you find it with the zero crossing
of the total_force) by comparing the voltages between the
out-most UFP.

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.hamiltonian
documentation:
Return the Hamiltonian at position z and dp in units of
Coul*Volt/p0.

Arguments:
    - make_convex: multiplies by sign(eta) for plotting etc.
      To see a literal 'bucket structure' in the sense of
      always having a local maximum in the Hamiltonian topology
      where the stable fix points are located, set
      make_convex=True in order to return
      sign(eta)*hamiltonian(z, dp).

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.equihamiltonian
documentation:
Return a function dp_at that encodes the equi-Hamiltonian
contour line that cuts the z axis at (zcut, 0).
In more detail, dp_at(z) returns the (positive) dp value at
its given z argument such that
self.hamiltonian(z, dp_at(z)) == self.hamiltonian(zcut, 0) .

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.separatrix
documentation:
Return the positive dp value corresponding to the separatrix
Hamiltonian contour line at the given z.

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.h_sfp
documentation:
Return the extremal Hamiltonian value at the corresponding
stable fix point (self.z_sfp_extr, 0) of the bucket.

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.dp_max
documentation:
Return the maximal dp value along the equihamiltonian which
is located at (one of the) self.z_sfp .

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.is_in_separatrix
documentation:
Return boolean whether the coordinate (z, dp) is located
strictly inside the separatrix of this bucket
(i.e. excluding neighbouring buckets).

If margin is different from 0, use the equihamiltonian
defined by margin*self.h_sfp instead of the separatrix.
(Use margin as a weighting factor in units of the Hamiltonian
value at the stable fix point to move from the separatrix
toward the extremal Hamiltonian value at self.z_sfp .)

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.make_is_accepted
documentation:
Return the function is_accepted(z, dp) definining the
equihamiltonian with a value of margin*self.h_sfp .
For margin 0, the returned is_accepted(z, dp) function is
identical to self.is_in_separatrix(z, dp).

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.emittance_single_particle
documentation:
The single particle emittance computed along a given
equihamiltonian line.

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.bunchlength_single_particle
documentation:
The corresponding RMS bunch length computed from the single
particle emittance.

methods.name: xpart.longitudinal.rf_bucket.RFBucket.guess_H0
documentation:
Pure estimate value of H_0 starting from a bi-Gaussian bunch
in a linear "RF bucket". Intended for use by iterative matching
algorithms in the generators module.

Holds a blueprint of the current RF bucket configuration.
Should be requested via RFSystems.get_bucket(gamma).

Contains all information and all physical parameters of the
current longitudinal RF configuration for a (real, not macro-)
particle.

Use for plotting or obtaining the Hamiltonian etc.

Warning: zmin and zmax do not (yet) account for phi_offset of
Kick objects, i.e. the left and right side of the bucket are not
accordingly moved w.r.t. the harmonic phase offset.
